title: The Rattrap by Selma Lagerlöf
subject: English

notes.heading: Theme
notes.text: Human goodness can reform a person. Compassion and dignity bring out the best in people.

summary:
The story is about a poor vagabond who earns a living by selling rattraps made from scrap wire. He is also a thief and lives a miserable, lonely life. He has a cynical philosophy: the whole world is a big rattrap. It offers baits like riches, food, shelter, and joy. Once someone is tempted, the trap closes and life becomes misery.

One evening he seeks shelter at a crofter’s cottage. The lonely old crofter welcomes him warmly, gives him porridge, plays cards with him, and even shows him 30 kronor earned by selling milk. The peddler steals the money the next morning. But then he gets lost in the forest, and he feels that the world-rattrap has caught him. Exhausted, he hears hammering and reaches Ramsjö Ironworks. The ironmaster mistakes him for Nils Olof, an old army comrade, and invites him home.

At the manor, the ironmaster’s daughter Edla sees that he is afraid and treats him with kindness and dignity, even after realizing he is not her father’s friend. She insists that he stay for Christmas Eve. That night the ironmaster discovers the theft and wants him thrown out, but Edla convinces her father to keep their promise of Christmas cheer.

Next morning, the peddler is gone. He leaves a rattrap as a Christmas gift for Edla, along with a note and the 30 kronor to be returned to the crofter. He signs as “Captain von Stahle,” because Edla treated him like a real captain. Her compassion gives him a chance to redeem himself.
